name: Malaria Vaccine Trial
description: Malaria infection outcomes for vaccine vs. placebo groups in a randomized trial. n=20.
studyDescription: In a randomized controlled trial, 20 volunteers were randomly assigned to receive either a malaria vaccine (n = 14) or a placebo (n = 6). All participants were then exposed to malaria-carrying mosquitoes, and researchers recorded whether each person became infected. This is a randomized experiment.

variableDescriptions.group: Whether the volunteer was randomly assigned to receive the malaria vaccine or the placebo.
variableDescriptions.outcome: Whether the volunteer became infected with malaria after exposure (Infected or Not infected).

sourceDetail: OpenIntro IMS, Chapter 17. Based on Lyke et al. (2017), a Phase 1 clinical trial of the PfSPZ malaria vaccine.

context.nullClaim: infection rate is the same for vaccine and placebo groups

inferenceContexts.parameter: the difference in infection-free rates between vaccine and placebo groups
inferenceContexts.claim: the malaria vaccine reduces infection rates compared to placebo
